summary: Iteration 21 - Recognition Score Feature & Backend Fix Complete. Backend was broken with NameError (apply_brown_bread_premium not defined) - fixed by replacing with correct function name (get_brown_bread_premium). Recognition Score feature working correctly - Taylor Swift shows score of 100 in autocomplete results.
passed_tests: GET /api/autocomplete?q=Taylor - Partial match returns celebrities with recognition_score
Recognition scores are within valid range (0-100)
GET /api/hot-celebs - Returns 8 hot celebrities with images
POST /api/celebrity/search - Taylor Swift returns A-LIST tier, £14.2M price
Frontend loads with Hot Celebs section
Search autocomplete shows recognition score badge (100 for Taylor Swift)
Celebrity detail modal opens with news and ADD TO TEAM button
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed NameError: 'apply_brown_bread_premium' not defined - replaced with 'get_brown_bread_premium' at lines 3370 and 3540
Recognition score correctly calculated from tier_score or tier_metrics stored in DB
context_for_next_testing_agent: Recognition Score feature is fully working. The autocomplete endpoint returns recognition_score for DB-matched celebrities (Taylor Swift = 100). The frontend SearchBar.jsx displays this as a small grey badge next to the tier badge. Fixed a NameError bug where apply_brown_bread_premium was called but the correct function name is get_brown_bread_premium.

bug_fix_details.fix: Replaced 'apply_brown_bread_premium' with 'get_brown_bread_premium' at lines 3370 and 3540 in server.py
bug_fix_details.verification: POST /api/celebrity/search now returns 200 with celebrity data

recognition_score_verification.display_location: SearchBar.jsx autocomplete dropdown - small grey badge next to tier badge
